title: Self-Improving Agents for Physical AI
description: AgenticSim proposal for runtime-governed Real2Sim2Real VLA evaluation
summary: Proposal for Alchedata: build AgenticSim, a runtime-governed Robot-for-Robot evaluation system that turns real-robot traces and failures into simulator tasks, reusable skill memories, eval-gated workflow improvements, and harder VLA benchmarks. The goal is not another static benchmark, but a closed-loop infrastructure where agents evaluate reality, learn from failures, improve safely in simulation, and return only verified changes to real robots.
asset_caption: Proposal frame: real execution failure -> trace memory -> eval gate -> world-model screening -> promoted agent / workflow / strategy -> harder diagnostic VLA eval.
details: Meeting framing for Alchedata, Tuesday 2026-05-26 17:00: propose AgenticSim as a joint infrastructure project for self-improving Physical AI, centered on runtime-governed Real2Sim2Real evaluation rather than a one-off simulator benchmark.

Problem: current VLA / robot evaluations often stop at sim scores, raw logs, or demo videos. They do not reliably answer whether a policy works on real robots, why it failed, whether the failure teaches a reusable skill memory, whether an agent workflow should change, or whether simulator improvements actually predict real-world outcomes.

Core thesis: self-improving Physical AI needs a verifiable loop: real robot or VLA execution produces traces and failures; agents attribute failures and distill trace-to-memory; simulator tasks, assets, and workflows are mutated; candidate policies / agents are evaluated under gates; world models screen risky changes before robot deployment; only versioned, reproducible improvements return to real-robot verification.

System sketch: Real trace capture -> typed failure attribution -> robotics Trace-to-Memory -> task / asset / workflow mutation -> Agentic Real2Sim2Real-VLA evaluation -> runtime governance gate -> real-robot replay / verification -> Self-Evolving VLA Evaluation that makes the next benchmark harder and more diagnostic.

Work package 1, Agentic Real2Sim2Real-VLA Evaluation: build an evaluation orchestrator around VLA policies, simulator tasks, real-robot traces, action logs, asset versions, seeds, and outcome metrics. Evaluation should test executability, world-model grounding, real-execution reliability, failure recoverability, and whether sim failures predict real failures.

Work package 2, Robotics Trace-to-Memory Distillation: convert raw robot execution traces into reusable skill memories. Initial failure taxonomy includes unstable grasp angle, object slip, excessive insertion force, motion-plan collision, contact-phase mismatch, and recovery failure. Output should be structured memory, not prose-only summaries.

Work package 3, Eval-Gated Workflow Evolution: let agents propose workflow, prompt, tool, controller, or task-generation changes, but promote them only through reproducible tests. A change is accepted when it improves target metrics without breaking safety, schema, replay, or simulator smoke gates.

Work package 4, World-Model-Guided Self-Improvement: use world models and generated object / scene physics assets to screen candidate improvements before risky robot trials. This is the imagination layer: useful for task mutation, counterfactual failures, object physics variation, and long-horizon rollouts.

Work package 5, Self-Evolving VLA Evaluation: avoid overfitting static benchmarks by generating harder diagnostic tasks from observed failures. The benchmark should evolve from failure memory and task-environment co-evolution, while preserving versioning and auditability.

Existing foundation already built: AgenticSim currently registers 129 Gymnasium environments: 9 official LeHome / LeHome-challenge household environments and the exact 120 formal RoboLab benchmark tasks. The task stack includes ChallengeGarmentLoader with four garment types and specialized folding detectors, and the current test suite passes 28/28.

MVP proposal: consolidate AgenticSim into one clean repository; keep a small failure-trace dataset; define the Robot-for-Robot output schema; add an Isaac Sim smoke gate and dependency check; ship a demo CLI that runs task generation, failure attribution, trace memory, eval gate, and report export end-to-end on a small task subset.

Robot-for-Robot output contract: every run should produce task spec, simulator seed, asset version, VLA checkpoint, action logs, real/sim outcome, failure attribution, trace memory, governance decision, artifact paths, and replay/verifier commands. The system should be legible to both humans and agents.

Conceptual references: PEARL supplies the self-improvement loop of typed failure memory, attribution, task-environment co-evolution, and runtime governance. RoboClaw is a lifecycle reference for VLM-driven collection, learning, deployment, monitoring, failure recovery, and human escalation. DreamDojo is a Real2Sim2Real world-model reference for learning from egocentric video and target robot data.

Concrete Alchedata discussion ask: align on the first target task family, the R4R data schema, the governance gates, what traces Alchedata cares about, and whether the first joint deliverable should be an AgenticSim demo repo, a proposal document, or a benchmark/report package.

Success criteria for the proposal: a reviewer should be able to run the demo, inspect every artifact, reproduce the gate decision, understand why a failure was attributed, and see how that failure creates a harder simulator task or a safer workflow improvement.
risks_decisions: Scope decision: start in simulation and trace replay, then return to real robots only after schema, replay, and safety gates are working.
Positioning decision: Alchedata is the active collaboration context; Periodic Labs is an external market signal for autonomous closed-loop labs, not a project partner.
Benchmark decision: emphasize the completed 129-environment foundation: 9 official LeHome / LeHome-challenge tasks plus 120 exact RoboLab formal benchmark tasks, not invented representative tasks.
Governance decision: runtime governance should be part of the learning signal, not only a safety wrapper. Agents should learn which proposed changes are rejected and why.
Schema risk: LeHome, LeHome Challenge, RoboLab, generated assets, and real-robot traces may expose incompatible task and observation schemas; the R4R contract must normalize them early.
Executability risk: generated tasks/assets must be physically valid, not only visually plausible. Isaac / simulator smoke gates are mandatory before a task enters evaluation.
Evaluation risk: if we only optimize simulator score, agents may overfit old tests. The system must check sim-to-real predictive validity and generate new diagnostic tasks from failures.
Safety risk: real-robot deployment is expensive and risky; world-model screening and eval gates should reduce, not replace, human review for risky physical changes.
Meeting decision needed: choose the first demonstration slice: trace-memory demo, Real2Sim2Real VLA eval demo, generated asset/task mutation demo, or full mini-loop CLI.

timeline.target_note: Meeting-ready proposal intro for runtime-governed AgenticSim and Robot-for-Robot evaluation.
